title: FOIA: Standard Records Request - Murray, UT

communications.communication:
March 30, 2026

Kim Sorensen
Murray

Re: Public Records Request — Holiday Lighting Procurement Records

Dear Kim Sorensen,

Pursuant to the Utah GRAMA (UCA § 63G-2), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from Murray:

[MISSING: recordsDescription].

This request covers [MISSING: fiscalYearRange].

As you may be aware, the Utah GRAMA requires a response within 10 days of receipt of this request.

I would prefer to receive these records in electronic format (PDF, Excel, or other digital format) delivered via email to rfp@britenites.com. If electronic delivery is not available, please advise on alternative arrangements.

Regarding fees: I request a fee waiver as this information is being sought in the public interest. If a fee waiver is not available, please provide a cost estimate before proceeding with the request if anticipated fees will exceed $25.00.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide any non-exempt portions that can be reasonably segregated.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at rfp@britenites.com if you have any questions or need clarification.

Sincerely,
Dillon Williams
rfp@britenites.com

title: Standard Records Request — City of Scottsdale

communications.communication:
March 30, 2026

To whom it may concern,
City of Scottsdale

Re: Public Records Request — Holiday Lighting Procurement Records

Dear To whom it may concern,,

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law (A.R.S. § 39-121), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from City of Scottsdale:

all records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services, including but not limited to: contracts, purchase orders, invoices, bid submissions, vendor proposals, requests for proposals (RFPs), requests for qualifications (RFQs), scope of work documents, vendor selection criteria, evaluation scoring sheets, and any correspondence with vendors or contractors related to holiday or seasonal lighting services.

This request covers the past five (5) fiscal years.

[MISSING: responseDeadlineParagraph]I would prefer to receive these records in electronic format (PDF, Excel, or other digital format) delivered via email to rfp@britenites.com. If electronic delivery is not available, please advise on alternative arrangements.

Regarding fees: I request a fee waiver as this information is being sought in the public interest. If a fee waiver is not available, please provide a cost estimate before proceeding with the request if anticipated fees will exceed $25.00.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide any non-exempt portions that can be reasonably segregated.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at rfp@britenites.com if you have any questions or need clarification.

Sincerely,
Dillon Williams
rfp@britenites.com

title: Standard Records Request — Denver

communications.communication:
March 30, 2026

Happy Haynes
Denver

Re: Public Records Request — Holiday Lighting Procurement Records

Dear Happy Haynes,

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act (CORA) (C.R.S. § 24-72-200.1), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from Denver:

all records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services, including but not limited to: contracts, purchase orders, invoices, bid submissions, vendor proposals, requests for proposals (RFPs), requests for qualifications (RFQs), scope of work documents, vendor selection criteria, evaluation scoring sheets, and any correspondence with vendors or contractors related to holiday or seasonal lighting services.

This request covers the past five (5) fiscal years.

As you may be aware, the Colorado Open Records Act (CORA) requires a response within 3 days of receipt of this request.

I would prefer to receive these records in electronic format (PDF, Excel, or other digital format) delivered via email to rfp@britenites.com. If electronic delivery is not available, please advise on alternative arrangements.

Regarding fees: I request a fee waiver as this information is being sought in the public interest. If a fee waiver is not available, please provide a cost estimate before proceeding with the request if anticipated fees will exceed $25.00.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide any non-exempt portions that can be reasonably segregated.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at rfp@britenites.com if you have any questions or need clarification.

title: Narrow Binford Road Property Seizure Records Binford Road

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All lists, records, notes, inventory forms, of all property seized from Bonnie Silveria, Elgio Limeta, or Sean Derning on Binford Road, between January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

All pictures associated with all lists, records, inventory forms, of property seized on Binford Road between from Bonnie Silveria, Elgio Limeta, or Sean Derning January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

All inventory forms, pictures, records, made by Julia Barnes or her subordinates, pertaining to property seized from Bonnie Silveria, Elgio Limeta, or Sean Derningon Binford Road between January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

title: Binford Road Inventory Forms

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All inventory forms, pictures, records, made by Julia Barnes or her subordinates, pertaining to property seized on Binford Road  between January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

Pursuant to the Utah GRAMA (UCA § 63G-2), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from Salt Lake City:

This request covers [MISSING: fiscalYearRange].

As you may be aware, the Utah GRAMA requires a response within 10 days of receipt of this request.

I would prefer to receive these records in electronic format (PDF, Excel, or other digital format) delivered via email to rfp@britenites.com. If electronic delivery is not available, please advise on alternative arrangements.

Regarding fees: I request a fee waiver as this information is being sought in the public interest. If a fee waiver is not available, please provide a cost estimate before proceeding with the request if anticipated fees will exceed $25.00.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide any non-exempt portions that can be reasonably segregated.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at rfp@britenites.com if you have any questions or need clarification.

Sincerely,
Dillon Williams

title: LIHTC & MTBA Documents

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: LIHTC & MTBA Documents
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

(1) A log or table of the LIHTC 9% competitive submissions/applications for 2018 (equivalent to the posting that exists for other years like 2020 at https://s3.amazonaws.com/thda.org/Documents/Business-Partners/Multi-Family-Developers/LIHTC-Program/2020-Competitive-Submissions-for-Website.pdf).
(2) The same kind log or table for the 2019 submissions. 
(3) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 2 of 2024 (equivalent to the posting, for round 1 of 2024, at https://dogvxws799i6n.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/MTBA-Round-1-Applications.pdf).
(4) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 1 of 2023.
(5) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 2 of 2023.
(6) A log or table of the MTBA Closings in 2025 (equivalent to the posting, for the year 2024, at https://dogvxws799i6n.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2024-MTBA-Listing-for-Website-Closing_Carryforward-1.pdf).

Note that the first five of these documents pertain to applications submitted, not awards allocated. For context, also note that this request was preceded by an attempt to locate these documents on the THDA website. Files were successfully located for other years, but not for those requested above. For #4, a document was found, but the version on the website does not include a THDA # (project ID), which is unusual. The documents sought in this request are expected to include this field, if it at all exists.

In case any of the six logs of applications/closings only appear as part of a larger spreadsheet or database extract that covers several years or rounds, such a record would also be considered valid toward satisfying this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Lars Brown.
